inflation themes contributing to a
further hawkish repricing of US dollar
money market curves the US dollar Index
is down 1% despite those supportive
Dynamics and threat of fresh tariffs
which have historically perpetuated US
dollar strength gold has absolutely
loved the reprieve in the US dollar
rallying 11% year to date as February
13th and outpacing the 3% year-to-date
return of Bitcoin our kiss and Dr Mo
signals remain Max along of both assets
all told there is an undeniable
broad-based down dollar trade going on
that is being funded by rotational flows
from US stocks in the crypto Market
could president Trump be using his
chaotic tariff threats as lever aage to
force key trading partners economic
Rivals alike to negotiate his version of
The Plaza Accord we cannot rule out a
globally coordinated decline in the US
dollar given president Trump's desire to
eliminate us trade imbalances by
restoring manufacturing and eliminating
us Reliance on foreign adversaries for
strategically important goods Global
equities and precious metal suggest you
should add a potential marago Accord to

obviously they can't touch national debt
and we know that's going to go up by

refinancing into a higher interest rate
regime um and in our view we we think

down uh just given the you know all the
expansion you know Manifest Destiny
across you know both North America
Greenland and ultimately this concept of
setting up you know Regional economic
and and defense zones that President
Trump has floated out I don't think
National defense is going to go down in
that situation it's probably going to go
up but I could be wrong on that but if
you assume that it's going to be flat
the four categories I just outlined
Medicare Social Security National
Defense and and that interest have been
growing at a compound 15% annual rate
over the past 3 years that's 65% of the
federal budget compounding get plus 65
or 15% on a trailing three-year K basis
now we know Social Security Medicare are
probably going to continue to go higher
as a function of our demographics in
terms of our old age dependency ratio
accelerating about 800 to a th000 basis
points over the next uh um 10 years uh
we know net interest is going to
continue to rise as our debt Rises and
as we continue to refinance you know
lower interest rate uh uh security
expiring lower interest rate Securities
with higher market-based interest rate
Securities and you know we can make have
a you know thoughtful discussion around
where net net National defense is going
but we're in a fourth turning and four
turning is where Total War tends to
happen so you know I'm not forecasting
Total War but I will tell you that
assuming National defense is going to go
down as we Crescendo into the climax
phase of this for turning seems to be a
a very I don't know very low probability
event in my opinion uh so we know 62
thirds of the federal budget is
compounding at plus 15% for
structural demographic reasons and and
just Market reasons that we can't
control so you have to cut the other
third essentially you know 35% of the
federal budget by more than that just to
stabilize you know spending just to
stabilize it and so when we go back to

obviously been very supportive of and

is we're going to be disappointed with

you know expenditure reduction we're
talking 500 billion to a trillion
dollars of of expenditures that we're
forecasting us which is represents of
significant amount but if you offset
that with further tax cuts uh you know
we believe in the laer Curve but who's
to say where we are in that laer curve
what we did last time with tax cuts
actually contributed to faster debt
growth so in my opinion we're probably
too far to the um you know we're not
where we need to be on the laer curve to
expect tax cuts to contribute to lower
deficits you know we're probably still
where we are where where tax cuts are
contributing to higher deficits in my
opinion just based on the most recent
example of the Trump tax cuts you know
you're probably going to line up with
the same or slightly lower deficits that
we currently are have and right now
we're tracking at minus 7% of nominal
GDP in a fully employed economy maybe we
get that down to five I don't see how we
get it down to the Scott bessin 3 and
and so ultimately um this process could
wind up being disappointing on a net
